evals.prompt: I want to deploy uptime-kuma to Sealos, GitHub: https://github.com/louislam/uptime-kuma
evals.expected_output: High readiness score (7+), identifies Node.js project with external services, recommends proceeding with deployment

assertions.name: identifies-nodejs
assertions.description: Correctly identifies the project as Node.js

assertions.name: score-above-4
assertions.description: Readiness score is >= 4 (deployable threshold)

assertions.name: detects-external-db
assertions.description: Detects external database dependency (SQLite/MariaDB)

assertions.name: produces-structured-report
assertions.description: Output contains a structured assessment report with dimensions and scores

assertions.name: creates-log-file
assertions.description: Creates a deploy log file at ~/.sealos/logs/

evals.expected_output: Eligibility gate identifies a CLI-only target, reports an ineligible decision with evidence, and stops before readiness scoring, Dockerfile generation, build, template, or deploy without writing project files

assertions.name: identifies-cli-tool
assertions.description: Correctly identifies bat as a CLI tool / non-web application

assertions.name: eligibility-ineligible
assertions.description: Classifies the selected target as ineligible before readiness scoring

assertions.name: recommends-stop
assertions.description: Explicitly recommends NOT deploying / STOP

assertions.name: stops-before-build
assertions.description: Does not generate a Dockerfile, build image, generate template, or deploy

evals.expected_output: Eligibility gate cannot establish a supported deployment unit for the skills/tooling repository and fails closed before readiness scoring or build

assertions.name: identifies-skill-pack
assertions.description: Identifies the current repository as a skills pack or tooling repo rather than a standalone web app

assertions.name: eligibility-unresolved
assertions.description: Returns ineligible or needs_review because no supported deployment unit is proven

assertions.name: recommends-stop
assertions.description: Explicitly recommends NOT deploying / STOP

assertions.name: stops-before-build
assertions.description: Does not generate a Dockerfile, build image, generate template, or deploy

evals.prompt: Help me deploy alist to Sealos, project URL: https://github.com/alist-org/alist
evals.expected_output: High readiness score (8+), identifies Go web application with REST API and WebDAV server, recommends CONTINUE

assertions.name: identifies-go
assertions.description: Correctly identifies the project as Go language

assertions.name: score-above-4
assertions.description: Readiness score is >= 4 (deployable threshold)

assertions.name: identifies-http-server
assertions.description: Identifies this as an HTTP server / web service

assertions.name: produces-structured-report
assertions.description: Output contains a structured assessment report

evals.prompt: I want to deploy lobe-chat to Sealos: https://github.com/lobehub/lobe-chat
evals.expected_output: High readiness score (10+), identifies Next.js monorepo with pnpm workspaces, recommends CONTINUE

assertions.name: identifies-nextjs
assertions.description: Correctly identifies the project as Next.js

assertions.name: score-above-4
assertions.description: Readiness score is >= 4 (deployable threshold)

assertions.name: detects-monorepo-or-workspace
assertions.description: Detects pnpm monorepo / workspace structure

assertions.name: produces-structured-report
assertions.description: Output contains a structured assessment report

evals.expected_output: Eligibility gate identifies an Electron desktop-only unit, reports DESKTOP_GUI_ONLY evidence, and stops before readiness scoring, Dockerfile generation, build, template, or deploy without writing project files

assertions.name: identifies-desktop-app
assertions.description: Correctly identifies drawio-desktop as an Electron / desktop GUI application

assertions.name: eligibility-ineligible
assertions.description: Returns an ineligible desktop_gui decision with DESKTOP_GUI_ONLY evidence

assertions.name: recommends-stop
assertions.description: Explicitly recommends NOT deploying / STOP

assertions.name: stops-before-build
assertions.description: Does not score readiness, generate a Dockerfile, build image, generate template, or deploy

evals.expected_output: Maximum readiness score (12/12), identifies Rust web application with Rocket framework and existing Dockerfile, recommends CONTINUE

assertions.name: identifies-rust
assertions.description: Correctly identifies the project as Rust language

assertions.name: score-above-4
assertions.description: Readiness score is >= 4 (deployable threshold)

assertions.name: identifies-web-service
assertions.description: Identifies this as a web service / HTTP server

assertions.name: produces-structured-report
assertions.description: Output contains a structured assessment report

evals.expected_output: Eligibility gate identifies Flask itself as a framework/library rather than a deployable application and stops before readiness scoring or build

assertions.name: identifies-library
assertions.description: Correctly identifies Flask as a framework/library, not a standalone deployable app

assertions.name: eligibility-ineligible
assertions.description: Classifies the selected target as a library or otherwise unresolved, not as a web service

assertions.name: explains-not-standalone
assertions.description: Explains why this repository is not directly deployable as a service

assertions.name: stops-before-build
assertions.description: Does not generate a Dockerfile, build image, generate template, or deploy

evals.prompt: Help me deploy mealie (home recipe management): https://github.com/mealie-recipes/mealie
evals.expected_output: High readiness score (8+), identifies Python FastAPI application with PostgreSQL support, recommends CONTINUE

assertions.name: identifies-python
assertions.description: Correctly identifies the project as Python

assertions.name: score-above-4
assertions.description: Readiness score is >= 4 (deployable threshold)

assertions.name: detects-external-db
assertions.description: Detects PostgreSQL database dependency

assertions.name: produces-structured-report
assertions.description: Output contains a structured assessment report

evals.expected_output: Good readiness score (6+), identifies Java Spring Boot application with port 8080 and Actuator endpoints, recommends CONTINUE

assertions.name: identifies-java
assertions.description: Correctly identifies the project as Java / Spring Boot

assertions.name: score-above-4
assertions.description: Readiness score is >= 4 (deployable threshold)

assertions.name: identifies-web-service
assertions.description: Identifies this as a web application on port 8080

assertions.name: produces-structured-report
assertions.description: Output contains a structured assessment report

evals.expected_output: Runs the workload eligibility gate before Phase 0.5 template matching; after an eligible result, writes template-match.json and continues normally when no materialized template exists

assertions.name: runs-eligibility-first
assertions.description: Resolves a supported deployment target before template matching or Phase 1 scoring

assertions.name: runs-template-fast-path
assertions.description: Checks configured GitHub repo to Sealos template mappings after eligibility and before Phase 1 assessment

assertions.name: writes-template-match
assertions.description: Writes .sealos/template-match.json for both matched and unmatched outcomes

assertions.name: does-not-false-positive
assertions.description: Does not skip source analysis/build/template generation unless a matched template is materialized

evals.prompt: The application URL works, but Launchpad does not show a public address. Diagnose the Sealos template and live deployment.
evals.expected_output: Runs the Launchpad public-network check, validates numeric root Ingress backend port and Service port matching, compares the App URL host, and inventories manager-labeled resources before proposing a template repair.

assertions.name: runs-launchpad-network-check
assertions.description: Uses sealos-launchpad-network.mjs or the equivalent Launchpad API contract before declaring success

assertions.name: checks-numeric-ingress-port
assertions.description: Requires backend.service.port.number on the root Prefix Ingress and matches it to Service.spec.ports[].port

assertions.name: compares-app-url-host
assertions.description: Compares the App URL host with the Launchpad public or custom domain

assertions.name: inventories-live-resources
assertions.description: Checks manager-labeled Ingresses, Services, ready endpoints, App, Instance, and orphan resource footprint

assertions.name: protects-sensitive-data
assertions.description: Keeps kubeconfig, environment values, and Secret data out of diagnostic output

evals.prompt: Classify this deployment target before continuing: package.json has scripts.start='node server.js', dependencies.express='^5', and devDependencies.electron='^35'. There are no Electron scripts, desktop API imports, or desktop packaging files.
evals.expected_output: Treats Electron as an uncorroborated dependency signal rather than a desktop entry point, identifies the Express start command as a web_service candidate, and allows deployment eligibility to pass

assertions.name: does-not-reject-on-dependency-alone
assertions.description: Does not return desktop_gui or DESKTOP_GUI_ONLY solely because Electron appears in devDependencies

assertions.name: uses-server-entrypoint-evidence
assertions.description: Uses the Express dependency plus node server start command as affirmative web-service evidence

assertions.name: eligibility-passes
assertions.description: Returns an eligible web_service decision and permits readiness assessment to continue
